asan.evidence_summary: Allocator accepted attacker-controlled size 4294967295, returned a non-NULL buffer, and ASan observed no crash.

lldb.evidence_summary: Printf fallback captured input_size=4294967295 reaching upb_Arena_Malloc() with a non-NULL allocation and successful boundary writes.

fresh.evidence_summary: Independent review also flagged the unbounded input_size allocation in DoTestIo() as a denial-of-service risk.

impact.evidence_summary: validation/impact_result.json was not present at analysis time.

notes: ASan and LLDB agree on the same runtime behavior: a peer-controlled 32-bit length reaches upb_Arena_Malloc() without bounds checks and can request a 4 GiB-scale allocation. Fresh validation also identified the same unbounded allocation issue. Impact validation was unavailable and was not scored.
